artPrompt: An endless coil of blank smudged parchment scrolls down a town-green folding table and pools across the grass, looping into the foreground in a foreshortened spiral while weary townsfolk bend over it with pens, the front signatories long aged into stooped silhouettes against golden light. Picket fences and ivory casserole tables recede into warm shadow, dust motes hanging. Rendered as a bold painted graphic-novel splash — strong silhouettes, heavy directional inking and dramatic shadow shapes, fully painted (not flat cel comic).
name: The Petition That Won't End
text:
When The Petition That Won't End enters the battlefield, every player must physically sign something — a napkin, the table, the air with a finger. The last player to finish signing creates no tokens this turn.
At the beginning of your upkeep, create a 1/1 green and white Voter creature token for each different player who has signed.
flavorText: Page 1,202. The recall has been going on so long the mayor it's recalling has retired, returned, and been recalled again.
